AdminAccounts.description: Accounts, keys and subscriptions at a glance (F1).

active_subscriptions.description:
Stripe-mirrored subscriptions in a paying state (`active` or
`trialing` — the same statuses the webhook treats as paid).

revoked_keys.description: API keys with `revoked_at` set (history is kept, never deleted).

AdminAgeBuckets.description: Age distribution of the open review queue (D1).

AdminAlertLatency.description:
Alert pipeline latency (F2): outbox created→dispatched percentiles plus
delivery created→sent percentiles. All figures are seconds.

dispatch_p50_seconds.description:
p50 of `dispatched_at - created_at` in seconds, real dispatches only;
`null` when no qualifying row exists.

dispatched_count.description: Dispatched events included in the percentiles (delta >= 1 second).

note.description: Why sub-1-second rows are excluded (documented approximation).

pre_dispatched_count.description:
Dispatched events EXCLUDED from the percentiles because their
created→dispatched delta is under 1 second — see `note`.

send_p50_seconds.description:
p50 of `updated_at - created_at` in seconds over sent deliveries;
`null` when nothing was sent yet.

sent_count.description: Deliveries with status `sent` backing the send percentiles.

AdminAttemptsBucket.description: One bar of the delivery attempts histogram (F3).

count.description: Deliveries with exactly that many attempts.

AdminBackfill.description: Section B in one round trip.

cloud_tasks_note.description: B5 Cloud Tasks caption: static note in v1.

completion.description: Historical completion per regime vs declared targets (B2).

fetch_density.description: Fetch density, last 48h by hour per source (B4).

fetch_density_note.description: B4 honesty caption: density is a proxy, not a fetch log.

freshness.description: Freshness + filing lag percentiles per source (B3).

generated_at.description: When this snapshot was assembled (server clock, UTC).

history_note.description: B1 honesty caption: pre-0011 history is log-only.

targets_note.description: B2 honesty caption: targets are declared, not discovered.

AdminBackfillRun.description:
One `backfill_run` row (migration 0011): what a worker bin did for one
year — status, counters, and what the budget gate decided.

bin.description: Worker bin that wrote the row, e.g. `backfill-real-br`.

budget.description: `BACKFILL_BUDGET` in force; `null` when no budget gate applied.

record_delta.description: Dry-run Gold-row delta the budget gate compared.

scope.description: Optional scope note, e.g. `--uf SP` or `nationwide`.

year.description: Archive / election year the run covered.

AdminBlockedJurisdiction.description: One blocked jurisdiction with its recorded reason (A1/A5).

blocked_reason.description: Why the factory blocked it; `null` when no reason was recorded.

AdminBrCollisionSweep.description:
br CPF collision sweep result (D3) — report-only, exactly like the
`check-br-identity-collisions` bin: zero rows = PASS, rows = investigate.

AdminBrCpfCollision.description: One politician whose `stg_br` rows carry more than one distinct CPF (D3).

distinct_cpfs.description: Distinct CPFs seen across the politician's filings.

AdminBudget.description: G1 — the monthly HARD CAP and the (unavailable) live spend.

hard_cap_usd.description: Monthly billing ceiling in USD, decimal string (never a float).

live_spend.description:
Live month-to-date spend in USD. Always `null` in v1 — no billing
export is wired; rendering a guess would violate fail-closed.

date.description: Journal entry date (the first `|`-separated field, trimmed).

AdminCoverage.description: `GET /v1/admin/coverage` — the full section-A payload, one round trip.

bronze_unbridged.description:
A3 honesty bucket: Bronze documents attributable to NO regime via the
bridge (no fetch run, or an adapter that never produced a bridged
filing) — surfaced, never silently dropped.

heatmap.description: A4: regime × year × `record_type` cells (rows with an `event_date`).

heatmap_missing_event_date.description:
A4 honesty bucket: Gold rows with NULL `event_date`, which no heatmap
cell can carry.

regimes.description: A2/A3/A5: per-regime rollup (every regime row, even zero-data ones).

alert_rule_id.description: The alert rule that fanned out.

last_error.description: Last error recorded by the dispatcher, verbatim.

outbox_event_id.description: The outbox event that triggered it.

updated_at.description: When the delivery last changed (the dead-lettering moment).

AdminDeliveries.description: Delivery health (F3): status×channel grid, attempts histogram, recent DLQ.

recent_dead.description: The 10 most recently dead-lettered deliveries with their errors.

AdminDeliveryStatusChannel.description: Delivery count for one (status, channel) cell (F3).

AdminDriftKindRow.description: Drift incidents for one kind (C3), across all regimes.

detections.description:
Total detections across all reports of this kind (re-detections bump
the counter instead of duplicating rows).

AdminEndpointCount.description: Request count for one endpoint (F1 top-endpoints board).

AdminEntityInventory.description:
Entity inventory (A6): reconciliation coverage plus the invariant-3
never-guess backlog.

instrument_isin_pct.description: Percent of instruments with an ISIN; `null` when there are none.

instrument_ticker_pct.description: Percent of instruments with a ticker; `null` when there are none.

politician_wikidata_pct.description:
Percent of politicians with a `wikidata_qid`; `null` when there are
no politicians (no denominator, no made-up 0%).

records_null_instrument.description:
Gold rows with NULL `instrument_id` — the invariant-3 backlog
(below-threshold matches stay NULL, never guessed).

AdminFailedRun.description:
One failed `pipeline_run` (C4) — raw error text, no fragile
classification.

finished_at.description: When the run finished, when recorded.

AdminFetchDensityBucket.description: One (regime, hour) fetch-density bucket (plan B4 politeness proxy).

AdminFreezeBoardRow.description:
One row of the adapter/freeze board (C1): sentinel state joined to its
open drift reports.

frozen.description: Whether the sentinel froze this regime's publication (design §5.6).

frozen_at.description: When the freeze happened, when recorded.

frozen_kind.description: Drift kind that froze it, when recorded.

last_checked_at.description: When the sentinel last probed this source.

worst_open_drift_kind.description: Kind of the highest-priority open drift, when any is open.

AdminFrozenRegime.description: One regime the sentinel froze (design §5.6 fail-closed publication gate).

frozen_at.description: When the freeze happened, when recorded.

frozen_kind.description: Drift kind that froze it (e.g. `layout_shift`), when recorded.

AdminFunnelRow.description:
One (adapter, stage) funnel cell (C2): run counts by status plus the
`PublishStats` rollup for publish-stage cells.

candidates.description:
Sum of `PublishStats.candidates`; `null` off the publish stage (the
jsonb keys only exist there — never guessed for other stages).

gold_inserted.description: Sum of `PublishStats.gold_inserted`; `null` off the publish stage.

outbox_written.description: Sum of `PublishStats.outbox_written`; `null` off the publish stage.

review_tasks.description: Sum of `PublishStats.review_tasks`; `null` off the publish stage.

runs.description: Total runs recorded for the cell.

suppressed.description: Sum of `PublishStats.suppressed`; `null` off the publish stage.

filings.description:
Filings discovered that day (`discovered_at` — `filing` has no
`created_at` column; discovery time is the honest ingestion clock).

AdminHeatmapCell.description: One cell of the regime × year × `record_type` heatmap (A4).

note.description: Scope caveat rendered verbatim on the page.

replayed_total.description: Sum of `replayed` — already-published filings re-runs left untouched.

AdminInfra.description: Section G — money & infra, static v1.

environment.description:
`cloud_run` when `K_SERVICE` is present in the process environment,
otherwise `local`.

queues.description: Cloud Tasks queue names as declared in terraform (G2).

queues_source.description: Provenance of `queues` — a file mirror, not a GCP read.

schedulers.description: Cloud Scheduler jobs as declared in terraform (G2).

schedulers_source.description: Provenance of `schedulers` — a file mirror, not a GCP read.

terraform_note.description: G3: terraform state is not surfaced in v1.

AdminLoop.description: Section H — autonomous-loop meta, one round trip.

budget_skips.description:
`BACKFILL_BUDGET skip:` trips from `agents/JOURNAL.md`, in file
order; `null` when the journal could not be read (an unreadable
journal is stated, not rendered as "no trips").

oneOf.description:
Git activity; `null` when any git subprocess failed (goals still
render — git absence is honest, not fatal).

repo_root.description: The repo checkout the metadata was read from (`GOVFOLIO_REPO_ROOT`).

AdminLoopGit.description: Git activity of the mounted checkout (H2).

branch.description: Current branch; `null` on a detached HEAD.

halted.description: The line mentions `HALT` without a `HALT RESOLVED` marker.

title.description: The rest of the line, verbatim.

AdminMimeCount.description: Bronze documents sharing one mime type (E1).

AdminOverview.description: The whole status strip in one round trip.

frozen_regimes.description: Regimes currently frozen by the sentinel; empty = nothing frozen.

generated_at.description: When this snapshot was assembled (server clock, UTC).

gold_records_estimate.description:
Planner ESTIMATE of Gold `disclosure_record` rows, straight from
`pg_class.reltuples` — free at any table size, never a `count(*)`
(this strip polls every 15s and nothing here may scan Gold). Kept
fresh by (auto)vacuum/analyze, so it drifts from the exact count;
`null` when postgres has never analyzed the table (honest absence,
not zero).

last_sentinel_check.description:
Latest `sentinel_watch.last_checked_at` across all regimes; `null`
when the sentinel has never run (honest absence, not zero).

top_tables.description: Top 25 tables by total relation size, largest first.

AdminPgTable.description: One of the 25 largest tables by total relation size (E3).

AdminPhaseCount.description: One `coverage_phase` bucket of the jurisdiction registry (A1).

AdminPipeline.description: Section C in one round trip.

conformance_note.description: C5 honesty caption: conformance is run locally, not recorded.

generated_at.description: When this snapshot was assembled (server clock, UTC).

supersede_activity.description: Supersede activity per month, newest first (C6).

regimes.description: Per-regime estimates; empty when no batch was drawn this month.

sample_month.description: The batch label, `YYYY-MM` (the database's current month).

AdminQuality.description: `GET /v1/admin/quality` — the full section-D payload, one round trip.

oneOf.description: D3: only present when the request opted in with `?sweep=br`.

open_by_reason.description: D1: open tasks by reason, most common first.

open_by_target_kind.description: D1: open tasks by target kind, most common first.

AdminQueueDepths.description:
Every operational queue depth on one row (plan B5). Shared verbatim with
`/v1/admin/backfill` so the two surfaces can never disagree on shape.

sample_pending.description: `sample_audit` rows still awaiting an audit verdict.

usage_unbilled.description: `usage_event` rows not yet folded into a `usage_report`.

linked_to_filing.description: Distinct documents referenced by at least one filing.

orphaned.description:
Documents no filing references (fetched but not yet promoted, or
evidence-only captures) — retained either way, Bronze is immutable.

AdminRegimeCompletion.description:
Historical completion for one regime (plan B2): what the data says vs the
declared v1 target.

missing_years.description: Target years with no filing data yet — the "what's left" list.

target_years.description:
Declared v1 target years (see `targets_note`); empty = no declared
target for this regime.

years_with_data.description:
Years with at least one filing on record (by `filing.filed_date` via
the provenance bridge; filings without a `filed_date` are not counted).

AdminRegimeCoverage.description: Per-regime coverage rollup (A2/A3/A5): window, tier counts, gap flags.

bronze_documents.description:
Bronze documents fetched by this regime's bridged adapter(s); `null`
when the regime is unbridged (count not attributable, never guessed).

built_not_backfilled.description:
A5 gap flag: the jurisdiction reached `built`/`live` but the regime
has zero Gold records — built, not yet backfilled.

first_filed_date.description: Earliest `filed_date` seen; `null` when no filing carries one.

last_filed_date.description: Latest `filed_date` seen; `null` when no filing carries one.

politicians.description: Distinct politicians with at least one filing under this regime.

record_types.description: Distinct Gold `record_type`s observed under this regime.

regime_codes.description:
Adapter regime codes provenance bridged to this regime
(`filing → raw_document → pipeline_run.adapter`); empty = unbridged
(e.g. fixture-seeded documents with no fetch run).

silver_rows.description:
Silver staging rows. Only `stg_us_house` and `stg_br` exist today, so
this is `null` for every other regime — a missing staging table is
reported as unavailable, not zero.

AdminRegimeFreshness.description:
Freshness of one source (plan B3): sentinel check, last fetch, last
discovery, and the filing lag percentiles.

lag_p50_seconds.description:
p50 of `discovered_at - published_at` in seconds; `null` when no
filing carries a `published_at`.

lag_p90_seconds.description:
p90 of `discovered_at - published_at` in seconds; `null` when no
filing carries a `published_at`.

last_fetched_at.description: Latest `raw_document.fetched_at` for this source's fetch runs.

sentinel_last_checked_at.description:
`sentinel_watch.last_checked_at`; `null` = the sentinel has never
probed this source.

AdminRegimePrecision.description: One regime's precision estimate in the current sampling batch (D6).

precision_estimate.description:
`(audited - discrepancies) / audited`; `null` until something is
audited (no estimate is invented from zero evidence).

AdminResolution30d.description:
30-day resolution throughput (D1), from `review_audit` applied attempts
joined to their tasks' `resolved_at`.

median_hours_to_resolve.description:
Median hours from task creation to resolution (`percentile_cont`);
`null` when nothing resolved in the window.

resolved_tasks.description: Distinct tasks resolved via an applied attempt in the last 30 days.

verdicts.description: Applied verdicts by kind in the window.

AdminRuns24h.description:
`pipeline_run` counts by status over the trailing 24 hours (by
`started_at`).

AdminScheduler.description:
One Cloud Scheduler job as declared in terraform (static mirror, not a
live GCP read).

paused.description:
Declared paused state (`true` for every job in v1 — unpausing is the
explicit go-live act).

AdminSchemeCount.description:
Bronze documents sharing one storage scheme (E1). Doubles as the
cloud-migration progress readout (`gs` vs `local`).

scheme.description: URI scheme of `storage_uri` (e.g. `gs`); plain paths report `local`.

AdminServing.description: Section F — serving & product, one round trip.

latency_note.description: Honest gap: per-request API latency is not recorded anywhere.

top_endpoints_7d.description: Top 10 endpoints by request count, last 7 days.

usage_by_day.description:
Authenticated requests per day, last 14 days (days with zero metered
requests are absent).

AdminStorage.description: `GET /v1/admin/storage` — the full section-E payload, one round trip.

bronze_by_mime.description: E1: Bronze documents by mime type, most common first.

bronze_by_scheme.description: E1: Bronze documents by storage scheme, most common first.

bronze_note.description: E1 caveat rendered verbatim on the page.

growth_30d.description:
E2: per-day growth over the last 30 days (days with zero activity on
both clocks are absent, not fabricated).

table_rows.description: Exact row counts of every schema table, largest first.

AdminSupersedeMonth.description:
Supersede activity for one month (C6, invariant-1 evidence): corrections
insert superseding rows, never update.

amended_filings.description:
Amended filings discovered that month (`supersedes_filing_id` set, by
`discovered_at`).

superseding_records.description:
`disclosure_record` rows inserted as supersessions that month (by
`created_at`).

AdminTableRowCount.description: Exact row count of one key table.

tasks.description: Open tasks with this target kind.

AdminUnlinkedInstruments.description: NULL-instrument Gold rows (D2) — the invariant-3 never-guess backlog.

listed.description:
The listed subset (asset classes equity/bond/fund/option) — the rows
a reference-data match could plausibly link.

AdminUsageDay.description: Authenticated API requests on one day (F1; `usage_event` rows).

AdminVerdictCount.description: Applied verdicts of one kind in the last 30 days (D1).

AlertChannel.description: One delivery channel of an alert rule (`alert_rule.channels` element).

oneOf.description:
HMAC-SHA256-signed webhook (design §6.4): the dispatcher POSTs JSON
and signs the exact body with `secret` in the
`X-Govfolio-Signature: sha256=<hex>` header.

secret.description: Per-rule shared secret for the HMAC signature.

channels.description: Delivery channels; at most one per channel type.

digest.description:
Digest mode: matches accumulate and ship as one periodic summary
instead of instant per-record sends.

filter.description:
Filter in the shared record grammar — identical to `/v1/records`
query parameters (design §6.3: one grammar, learned once).

updated_at.description: When the rule was last updated.

user_id.description: Owning account (`user_account.id` — always the authenticated caller).

AlertRuleSpec.description:
Create/replace body for an alert rule (`POST` and `PUT` share it). The
owner is always the authenticated account — never caller-supplied.

active.description: Whether the rule matches at all (defaults to active).

channels.description: Delivery channels; at least one, at most one per channel type.

filter.description: Filter in the shared record grammar; unknown keys are rejected.

ApiKeyInfo.description: One key as listed — metadata only, nothing secret-derived.

revoked_at.description: When the key was revoked; `null` while active.

BackfillProgress.description: Backfill progress across every regime that has at least one filing.

regimes.description: Per-regime progress, ordered by regime id.

label.description: Human label for the key (shown in listings).

user_id.description:
Target user — REQUIRED with admin auth, FORBIDDEN with key auth
(a key only mints keys for its own account).

CreatedKey.description: The creation response — the ONLY place the plaintext key ever appears.

key.description:
The bearer token (`gfk_...`). Shown once; store it now — only its
hash is retained.

Currency.description:
ISO 4217 currency code (`char(3)` in DDL), uppercase on the wire — `snake_case`
would mangle codes. Closed set for now; extend as regimes land (visible in the
schema snapshot).

alert_rule_id.description: The alert rule it belonged to.

DeliveryHealth.description:
Alert-delivery health: ledger counts, 24h throughput, outbox backlog and
the recent DLQ.

oldest_undispatched_at.description: Oldest undispatched event's creation time (backlog age).

sent_24h.description: Deliveries sent in the trailing 24 hours.

DisclosureRecord.description:
One canonical disclosure record (Gold `disclosure_record`, design §4.2).
`verification_state` is present on EVERY record — honesty travels with
the data.

asset_description_raw.description: Asset description exactly as filed, always kept (invariant 2).

created_at.description: When the record row was created.

details.description:
Contract-typed regime payload (validated per (regime, `record_type`)
JSON Schema at promotion, invariant 5).

extracted_by.description: Parser id / model+prompt version that produced this record.

filing_id.description: Source filing this record came from.

instrument_id.description: Resolved instrument — `null` below threshold, never guessed (invariant 3).

record_type.description: One of the four observation types.

regime_id.description: Disclosure regime the record was filed under.

supersedes_record_id.description: Superseded record in a correction chain (supersede, never update).

oneOf.description: Declared value band; bounds are decimal STRINGS (invariant 7).

verification_state.description: Two-stage publication state (design §7.1) — on every record.

detections.description: How many times the same open anomaly re-detected.

priority_score.description: Severity rank — the orchestrator picks the worst first.

review_task_id.description: The auto-filed review task, when one was opened.

ErrorBody.description: The error envelope every non-2xx response carries.

ErrorDetail.description: Machine-readable error code plus a human-readable message.

ExtractionCacheInfo.description:
Extraction-cache evidence for the task's document: which model produced
the cached extraction and how (`provenance` carries the cross-check
status when the live call recorded one). Latest entry for the document's
sha + the record's extractor tag; absent for deterministic (non-LLM)
parses.

model_id.description: Model that produced the cached extraction.

provenance.description:
How the entry was produced (audit surface): source, models, and
`cross_checked` verdict when a second model re-extracted (design §5.3).

ExtractionContext.description:
The LLM pre-review note (design §7.2): who/what extracted the target
record, with what confidence, and the extraction-cache evidence when the
LLM seam produced it. The record's Silver staging payload is NOT included
yet (see the module-level silver-payload gap note).

oneOf.description:
Cache evidence for LLM-extracted documents; `null` for deterministic
parses.

extracted_by.description: Parser id / model+prompt version that produced the record.

ExtractionCostMonth.description: One month of LLM extraction spend.

extraction_runs.description: Parse-stage runs that recorded a `stats.extraction` block.

ExtractionCostReport.description:
Monthly LLM extraction spend vs the HARD CAP.

COST INTERFACE CONTRACT with goal 021 Phase 2: parse-stage
`pipeline_run.stats.extraction` is expected to carry
`{tokens_in, tokens_out, estimated_cost_usd (decimal string), passes}`.
Nothing writes that block yet — every month reports zeros until 021 lands
it (the SQL is null-tolerant by design). 021 owners must adopt these key
names or update [`EXTRACTION_COSTS_SQL`].

hard_cap_usd.description: The monthly HARD CAP, decimal string — always `"200.00"`.

months.description:
Months, oldest first — every requested month present, zeros when
nothing was spent.

ExtractionMonth.description:
Current-month LLM extraction spend vs the HARD CAP. Zeros until goal 021
Phase 2 starts writing `pipeline_run.stats.extraction` (see
[`extraction_costs`] for the key contract).

cap_utilization_pct.description: Spend as a percentage of the cap (derived display number, not money).

hard_cap_usd.description: The monthly HARD CAP, decimal string — always `"200.00"`.

FilingProvenance.description: Filing provenance: the source filing the record came from.

external_id.description: Source-native filing id when the source has one.

published_at.description: When the government made it public.

sources.description: Every watched source's baseline (freeze state included).

Healthz.description:
Liveness probe body: process is up; `db` reports pool reachability.
Deliberately carries NOTHING sensitive (served without auth).

status.description:
`ok` when the process and its database both answer; `degraded` when
the database does not.

Jurisdiction.description: One jurisdiction with its disclosure regimes — the scorecard row.

coverage_phase.description:
Coverage-factory phase (design §5.8): `stub` | `scouted` | `surveyed` |
`sampled` | `specced` | `built` | `live` | `blocked`. `live` = a built
adapter is ingesting; a jurisdiction whose only regime is `type = 'none'`
is a `stub` awaiting research.

id.description: Stable jurisdiction id (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 lowercase by convention).

priority_score.description:
Coverage-factory work-ordering score within the epoch (design §5.8);
`null` until the factory scores it.

regimes.description:
Disclosure regimes of this jurisdiction, in id order — the
transparency-scorecard metadata (design §7.3). A `regime_type = 'none'`
row is a stub (no researched regime yet); any other type is live.

LastActivity.description: Last-seen timestamps — the "is anything alive" pulse row.

Mandate.description: One mandate (Gold `mandate`): a role held in a jurisdiction body.

start_date.description: Mandate start (roster-attested "active since at least" bound).

OpsOverview.description:
The composite overview panel — one cheap body per poll (5–15s cadence),
high `ETag` 304 hit-rate when nothing moved.

extraction_month.description: Current-month extraction spend vs the HARD CAP.

OpsTotals.description: Whole-system totals (design §4.2 tables, one count each).

frozen_regimes.description: Regimes currently frozen by the sentinel (design §5.6 fail closed).

silver_rows.description: Silver staging rows across the per-regime `stg_*` tables.

Owner.description:
`owner` CHECK: whose asset the record concerns. `self` is a Rust keyword,
hence the `Self_` variant with an explicit wire rename.

id.description: Run ULID, minted at claim — time-ordered, the pagination cursor.

PipelineRunPage.description: One page of runs, newest first.

items.description: Runs in descending id (= claim-time) order.

next_cursor.description: Pass back as `cursor` for the next (older) page; `null` at the end.

PoliticianPage.description: One page of politicians; the same `next_cursor` rule as `/v1/records`.

next_cursor.description: Pass back as `cursor` to fetch the page after this one; `null` at the end.

PoliticianProfile.description:
A politician profile: the person, their mandates, and a record summary
(the profile page's above-the-fold data, design §6.4).

Provenance.description:
Full provenance of one record: filing, archived raw document, and the
regime it was filed under (with its methodology metadata).

raw_document.description: Our archived copy of the official document.

regime.description: The disclosure regime the record was filed under.

RawDocumentProvenance.description:
Raw-document provenance: our archived copy of the official document
(invariant 2: raw is sacred — official link + archived copy, design §7.3).

sha256.description: sha256 of the archived Bronze bytes (integrity + citability).

source_url.description: Official source URL the document was fetched from.

RecordDetail.description:
One record with its trust surface (design §7.3): provenance plus the full
supersession history in both directions (invariant 1: corrections insert
superseding rows, so history is a chain of records).

superseded_by.description: Corrections that (transitively) supersede this one, ascending ULID.

supersedes.description:
Records this one (transitively) supersedes — the corrected history,
ascending ULID (= insertion-time) order.

RecordFilter.description:
A conjunctive filter over `disclosure_record` (design §6.1 filter list:
jurisdiction, type, `asset_class`, instrument, politician, date range,
value bounds, `verification_state`). Every field is optional; an empty
filter matches everything.

Value-bound semantics: a record matches when its declared band
`[value_low, value_high|∞]` OVERLAPS `[value_min, value_max]`; records
without a declared value never match a value bound.

oneOf.description: Only records of this asset class.

event_date_from.description: Only records with `event_date` on or after this date.

event_date_to.description: Only records with `event_date` on or before this date.

oneOf.description:
Only records resolved to this instrument (below-threshold matches are
NULL and never match — invariant 3).

jurisdiction_id.description:
Only records filed under a regime of this jurisdiction
(`jurisdiction.id`, e.g. `us`).

value_max.description:
Only records whose declared value band can stay at this amount or less
(decimal string — invariant 7).

value_min.description:
Only records whose declared value band can reach this amount or more
(decimal string — invariant 7).

oneOf.description: Only records in this verification state.

RecordPage.description:
One page of records; `next_cursor` is the last item's ULID, `null` when
the listing is exhausted (design §6.1: cursor pagination on ULIDs).

items.description: Records in ascending ULID (= insertion-time) order.

next_cursor.description: Pass back as `cursor` to fetch the page after this one; `null` at the end.

RecordSummary.description: Aggregate summary of a politician's disclosure records.

RecordType.description: `record_type` CHECK: the four observation types, one table (design D1).

Regime.description:
One disclosure regime — a jurisdiction body's disclosure rules and the
transparency-scorecard metadata (design §6.1/§7.3).

disclosure_lag_days.description: Statutory maximum disclosure lag in days.

effective_from.description: Date the regime's rules took effect.

effective_to.description: Date the rules stopped applying; `null` while current.

jurisdiction_id.description: Jurisdiction the regime belongs to (`jurisdiction.id`, e.g. `us`).

regime_code.description:
Adapter regime code (`us_house`, `br`, ...); `null` when no static or
publish-derived mapping exists for the regime row (module docs).

regime_id.description:
Seeded `disclosure_regime.id`. Deliberately un-patterned:
`disclosure_regime.id` is unconstrained text (registry- and
adapter-seeded ids both exist), so publishing a ULID pattern here
would be a lie and `.*` would be dead weight.

stages.description:
Per-stage run progress for the regime's adapter code (shared across
regime rows of a multi-body adapter, e.g. both br chambers).

years.description: Year-by-year progress, oldest first; the `null` year bucket last.

bronze_documents.description: Distinct Bronze documents that produced filings for this regime.

review_open.description:
Open review tasks attributed to this regime (three target kinds:
record → `regime_id` join, bare filing id → `filing` join, and
code-keyed `regime`/`filing` targets — code-keyed counts repeat on
every regime row sharing the adapter code, e.g. both br chambers).

silver_rows.description:
Silver staging rows, attributed per DOCUMENT: a document shared by
filings of more than one regime (e.g. one br CSV feeding both
chambers) reports its full row count under each — Silver rows carry
no regime linkage of their own.

ResolveRequest.description:
Resolve request: reviewer identity plus one verdict. `regime_code` and
`corrected` travel only with `verdict = "edit"` (they select the details
contract and carry the corrected facts); supplying them with any other
verdict fails closed. Corrected identity fields (`filing_id`,
`politician_id`, `regime_id`) may be omitted — promote pins identity from
the original row and ignores reviewer-supplied values.

corrected.description: Corrected facts for an edit, in the `GoldCandidate` wire shape.

note.description: Optional note, recorded verbatim in the audit log.

reviewer.description: Reviewer identity — free text until accounts land (goal 050).

ResolveResponse.description:
What an applied resolution did (non-applied attempts surface as errors:
`409` for an already-resolved task, `5xx` for a failed resolution).

superseding_record_id.description:
The superseding `corrected` record an edit inserted (`null` for
confirm/reject).

ResolvedDay.description:
Tasks resolved per UTC day (any terminal status — `resolved_at` is
stamped for dismissals too).

ReviewAuditEntry.description:
One resolve attempt in the audit log — exactly one row per attempt,
whatever came of it.

affected_record_ids.description: Record ids the attempt touched (`[]` for non-applied attempts).

reviewer.description: Who attempted (free text until goal 050 auth).

ReviewHealth.description:
Review-queue health: status counts, open-by-reason, 14-day resolution
series, and the monthly sampling-audit precision source.

oldest_open_at.description: Creation time of the oldest still-open task.

open_by_reason.description: Open tasks rolled up by reason, largest first.

resolved_by_day.description: Tasks resolved per UTC day over the trailing 14 days.

ReviewQueueItem.description:
One queue entry: the task plus its target-record summary (`null` when the
task targets something other than a disclosure record).

ReviewQueuePage.description:
One page of the review queue, ranked `priority_score` desc, then
`created_at` asc (oldest first within a priority), then id.

next_cursor.description:
Pass back as `cursor` to fetch the page after this one; `null` at the
end. Ranking (not id order) is preserved across pages.

ReviewTargetSummary.description: Target-record summary on a queue item — the reviewer's scan surface.

extracted_by.description: Parser id / model+prompt version that produced the record.

politician_name.description: Canonical name of the politician the record concerns.

record_type.description: One of the four observation types.

oneOf.description: Declared value band; bounds are decimal STRINGS (invariant 7).

id.description: Task ULID (time-sortable; the queue pagination cursor).

priority_score.description: Queue rank: impact × uncertainty (design §7.2); higher reviews first.

ReviewTaskDetail.description:
One review task with its full adjudication surface: the target record in
the public trust shape (provenance + supersession history) and the
extraction context.

oneOf.description: The LLM pre-review note; present when the target record is.

Full target record (the 040a `RecordDetail` shape) for
`disclosure_record` tasks.

RunGroup.description: One (adapter, stage, status) rollup over the window.

p50_seconds.description:
Median run duration in seconds (finished runs only; `null` when none
finished — `percentile_cont` ignores null `finished_at` durations).

p95_seconds.description: 95th-percentile run duration in seconds (finished runs only).

RunSeriesPoint.description: One (bucket, adapter) point of the throughput series.

bucket_start.description: Bucket start (UTC, `date_trunc` of the requested bucket).

failed.description: Runs finished `failed` in the bucket (by start time).

succeeded.description: Runs finished `succeeded` in the bucket (by start time).

Runs24h.description:
Pipeline-run pulse over the trailing 24 hours (running counts are global,
not window-bounded — a run started days ago and still `running` is stale
wherever it started).

stale_running.description: Runs `running` for over an hour — likely crashed before finish.

RunsSummary.description:
Windowed run rollup: per-(adapter, stage, status) groups with duration
percentiles, plus a bucketed throughput series.

window_hours.description: The trailing window the rollup covers, in hours.

SampleAuditSlice.description: One month × regime slice of the sampling audit (design §7.4 precision).

regime_id.description: Gold regime the sample was drawn from.

SearchResults.description:
Typed search results: one arm per entity kind, each capped at 20 hits in
id order.

instruments.description: Instruments whose name or ticker contains the query.

politicians.description: Politicians whose canonical name or as-filed alias contains the query.

SentinelSource.description: One sentinel per-source baseline row (`sentinel_watch`, design §5.6/§5.8).

frozen.description: Whether the regime's publication is currently frozen (fail closed).

frozen_kind.description: The drift kind that froze it.

last_checked_at.description: When the sentinel last probed the source.

last_layout_hash.description: Last structural layout hash of the listing markup.

regime_code.description: Adapter regime code the sentinel watches.

StageProgress.description:
Stage progress for the regime's adapter code. Failure attribution is
regime+stage level only — run idempotency keys carry `sha`/`external_id`, no
year (plan "Non-obvious query decisions").

Tier.description:
`user_account.tier` CHECK (design §6.2 freemium table): the tier decides
freshness (free = 24h-delayed via the ONE visibility bound in
`core::query`) and the daily request quota. Default is `free`.

tier.description: Tier (decides freshness delay + daily quota, design §6.2).

ValueInterval.description:
A money interval `low ..= high` in `currency`. Exact figures are `low == high`;
`high == None` is an open-ended threshold (UK "over £70,000"). Construction is
the only door: `high < low` never exists — in memory or on the wire, because
the manual `Deserialize` impl funnels back through [`ValueInterval::new`].

documents.description: Distinct Bronze documents behind those filings.

politicians_with_filings.description: Distinct politicians with at least one filing that year.

roster_members.description:
Roster denominator: distinct politicians holding a mandate on the
regime's `(jurisdiction_id, body)` during that year (`mandate.body`
values are written from the same adapter constants as
`disclosure_regime.body`, so the join is exact). `null` for the
unknown-year bucket.

year.description: Filing year; `null` = filings whose `filed_date` is unknown.

info.description: Worldwide politician financial-disclosure tracking. Cursor pagination on ULIDs; consistent error envelope; verification_state on every record. Every successful GET carries a strong ETag (sha256 of the body); requests with a matching If-None-Match receive 304 Not Modified. Authentication: `Authorization: Bearer gfk_...` API keys (create via /v1/keys; hashes only at rest). Requests without a key are served at the free tier. Tiers control record freshness (free: filings we discovered less than 24 hours ago are not yet visible; pro/data: real time) and the daily request quota (counted per account; over quota is 429 quota_exceeded in the standard envelope). Anonymous traffic has a light per-IP rate backstop (429 rate_limited).
info.title: govfolio API

get.summary:
Liveness probe. Unauthenticated and un-ETagged by mounting position
(added AFTER the middleware layers in `lib.rs` — axum layers only wrap
routes added before them). Always `200`; database trouble is reported in
the body, not as a 5xx, so the probe distinguishes "process dead" (no
answer) from "db unreachable" (degraded).

get.description:
# Errors
`401` without a valid `X-Admin-Token` (the gate wraps the whole admin
subtree); `500` on backend failure — all in the consistent envelope.
get.summary:
Section B — backfill & ingestion: run history, completion vs declared
targets, per-source freshness + lag percentiles, the fetch-density
politeness proxy, and queue depths — one round trip.

200.description: Coverage & inventory: phases, blocked list, per-regime rollup, heatmap, entity inventory

get.summary: Section-A coverage & inventory snapshot (admin dashboard `/admin/coverage`).

get.description:
# Errors
Same as [`get_filing_document`], plus `401`/`403` from the admin gate.

200.description: The archived document bytes; Content-Type reflects the sniffed mime type

503.description: Storage backend not available for this document

get.summary:
Serves the archived original document for one filing, in REAL TIME
(admin-gated): the reviewer surface must see freshly-ingested filings the
moment they exist, not 24h later — mirrors `review::get_review_task`'s
own `RecordFilter::default()` use for exactly the same reason. Consumed
by the web app's same-origin reviewer document proxy, never called
directly from a browser (the `X-Admin-Token` never reaches client JS).

Money & infra observability (section G), static v1: HARD CAP, terraform
scheduler/queue mirror, environment badge. Touches no GCP APIs and never
errors locally.

get.description:
# Errors
`503` when `GOVFOLIO_REPO_ROOT` is not configured (the cloud posture — by
design); `500` when the goal index cannot be read or parsed — consistent
error envelope.
get.summary:
Autonomous-loop meta (section H): goal queue with HALT detection, git
activity, guardrail trips. Only available where the repo checkout is
mounted (`GOVFOLIO_REPO_ROOT`).

get.description:
# Errors
`401` outside the admin gate; `500` on backend failure.

parameters.description:
Adapter regime-code filter, e.g. `us_house` (matches the resolved
`regime_code`; a multi-body adapter returns all its regime rows).
parameters.name: adapter

get.summary:
Per-regime, per-year backfill progress: filings/documents/gold split by
filing year, politicians-vs-roster coverage, stage-level run outcomes.

get.summary: Alert-delivery health (design §6.3): ledger counts, outbox backlog, DLQ.

200.description: Monthly extraction spend vs the HARD CAP

get.description:

get.summary: Review-queue health (design §7.1–7.2, §7.4).

get.description:
# Errors
`400` on a malformed status, cursor or limit; `401` outside the admin
gate; `500` on backend failure.

parameters.description: Only runs started at/after this instant (RFC 3339).

get.summary: Windowed run rollup with duration percentiles and a throughput series.

The admin status strip: queue depths, 24h run counts, frozen regimes,

get.description:

`401` without a valid `X-Admin-Token` (the gate wraps the whole admin

get.description:
# Errors
`400` on an unsupported `sweep` value; `401` without a valid
`X-Admin-Token` (enforced by the route-layer gate); `500` on backend
failure — all in the consistent error envelope.

parameters.description:
Opt-in expensive sweep. The only supported value is `br` (the CPF
collision sweep); anything else is a 400.
parameters.name: sweep

200.description: Data quality & review ops: queue analytics, unlinked backlog, precision, idempotency, raw retention, optional br sweep

200.description: Storage & tiers: Bronze counts by mime/scheme, exact table rows, 30d growth, Postgres physical stats

delete.summary:
Revokes a key (immediate — the very next request with it is `401`).
Key auth revokes own keys; `X-Admin-Token` revokes any.

post.description:
# Errors
`400` on a malformed body; `404` for an unknown task; `409` when the task
is already resolved; `500` when the resolution fails closed (e.g. a
correction violating the details contract) — the attempt is still
audit-logged.

400.description: Malformed reviewer, verdict or corrected payload

500.description: The resolution failed closed and rolled back whole

post.summary:
Resolves one review task through the pipeline promote path (the single
write authority; design §7.2). Every attempt that carries a verdict is
audit-logged — applied, conflicting and failed alike.

get.description:

consistent error envelope.

character; `%`/`_` are literals.
parameters.name: q

tags.description: Archived original filing documents (design §7.3: our own durable copy, not just a link to the government's site). Same freshness gate as records.
tags.name: filings

tags.description: Jurisdictions and disclosure regimes — the transparency scorecard (design §6.1/§7.3)
tags.name: jurisdictions

tags.description: Minimal substring search over politicians and instruments (Postgres-backed until it hurts, §6.4)
tags.name: search

tags.description: Alert rules over the shared record filter grammar (design §6.3). Requires a pro or data tier API key; rules belong to the authenticated account.
tags.name: alert-rules

tags.description: Accounts and API keys (goal 050). Account creation is operator-bootstrap behind X-Admin-Token until self-serve signup ships; keys are hashed at rest and shown exactly once.
tags.name: account

tags.description: Stripe integration seam (goal 050): signature-verified webhook mirroring subscription status; usage metering flows from usage_event via the billing-sync worker.
tags.name: billing

tags.description: Admin review queue (design §7.1–7.2), X-Admin-Token gated: priority-ranked tasks with REAL-TIME record context and extraction evidence; resolutions go through the pipeline promote path (supersede-never-update) and every attempt is audit-logged.
tags.name: review

tags.description: Admin observability dashboard (X-Admin-Token gated, READ-ONLY): nine composite snapshots — overview status strip, coverage & inventory, backfill & ingestion, pipeline health, data quality & review ops, storage & tiers, serving & product, money & infra (static v1), and autonomous-loop meta (503 without GOVFOLIO_REPO_ROOT, by design). Metrics that cannot be observed are explicit unavailable states, never guessed.
tags.name: admin

tags.description: Read-only admin ops observability (goal 090), X-Admin-Token gated: backfill progress, pipeline runs, sentinel freezes/drift, review-queue health, alert delivery and LLM extraction cost vs the USD 200.00 monthly HARD CAP. Poll-friendly: every body carries a strong ETag. Narrower and largely superseded by the `admin` tag's read surface; kept for /healthz and its own index migration.
tags.name: ops

tags.description: Liveness probe: unauthenticated, un-ETagged, carries nothing sensitive.
tags.name: health
